response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has strong general software engineering credentials spanning 15+ years, but is a poor fit for this role on almost every critical axis. The hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency is not met — he speaks English, Romanian, and basic German. There is no evidence of security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption), no embedded networking or LAN-switch experience, and no Linux kernel-level work. His C/C++ exposure is limited to early-career and side projects; his career trajectory is firmly in web, cloud, and TypeScript/Node.js. None of the preferred embedded skills (chip SDKs, BIOS/bootloader, SNMP, NETCONF) appear anywhere in the resume.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems
Early-career C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in academic/personal projects
Strong English proficiency for reading technical documentation and written communication
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role
No security domain experience: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, or attack detection work
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDK experience
